ai.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
ai.snippet: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

ai.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
ai.snippet: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.

ai.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
ai.snippet: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

ai.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
ai.snippet: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

ai.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
ai.snippet: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

ai.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
ai.snippet: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…

ai.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
ai.snippet: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

ai.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
ai.snippet: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

ai.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
ai.snippet: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

ai.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
ai.snippet: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.

ai.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
ai.snippet: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

ai.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
ai.snippet: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

ai.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
ai.snippet: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

ai.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
ai.snippet: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

ai.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
ai.snippet: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

ai.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
ai.snippet: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

ai.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
ai.snippet: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

ai.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
ai.snippet: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

ai.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
ai.snippet: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

ai.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
ai.snippet: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

ai.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
ai.snippet: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

ai.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
ai.snippet: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

ai.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
ai.snippet: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

ai.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
ai.snippet: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the Jacobian lens (or…

ai.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
ai.snippet: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

ai.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
ai.snippet: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

ai.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
ai.snippet: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

ai.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
ai.snippet: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

ai.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
ai.snippet: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

ai.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
ai.snippet: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

ai.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
ai.snippet: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

ai.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
ai.snippet: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

ai.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
ai.snippet: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

ai.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
ai.snippet: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

ai.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
ai.snippet: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

ai.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
ai.snippet: In an interesting twist that takes advantage of the company's core product, users can chat with these shows' characters, ask them questions, and even roleplay different storylines.

ai.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
ai.snippet: Nilekani remains Fundamentum's anchor investor as the firm expands its leadership team and targets AI and fintech startups in India.

cloud.title: Amazon SQS turns 20: Two decades of reliable messaging at scale
cloud.snippet: On July 13, 2006, we launched Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) as one of the first three services available to customers, alongside Amazon EC2 and Amazon S3. We had learned firsthand that distributed systems need a reliable way to pass messages between components without creating tight dependencies. If one service called another directly and […]

cloud.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: AWS Builder Center at 1 year, Network Scanning in Security Hub, Loom for AWS, and more (July 13, 2026)
cloud.snippet: AWS Builder Center turned one year old last week. Launched on July 9, 2025, the platform has grown from a community hub with Wishlist voting, community profiles, and a toolbox into a full ecosystem with sandbox environments, workshops, Spaces, and a Builders’ Library. To mark the anniversary, Rick Suttles published a full feature timeline covering […]

cloud.title: Key findings from the 2026 Public Sector M-Trends report and beyond
cloud.snippet: In 2026, the public sector is no longer defending a traditional perimeter. Instead, they are defending a complex web of interconnected trust relationships against adversaries that now operate at machine speed. We recently published the 2026 Public Sector Threat Landscape: M-Trends and Beyond report, which distills more than 500,000 hours of frontline incident investigations conducted by Mandiant i

cloud.title: Building the AI-defined vehicle with Android, Google Cloud, and Nexus SDV
cloud.snippet:
The automotive industry is moving from building hardware-centric platforms toward building their own sophisticated Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV) architectures. For OEMs, a vehicle is no longer just a way to go from point A to point B, but an intelligent, connected node within an AI-native ecosystem! 
With its partners, Google’s Android and Google Cloud are at the forefront of this transition. And

cloud.title: Contributing to U.K. financial sector resilience as a critical third party
cloud.snippet:
At Google Cloud, we take our role in the financial ecosystem very seriously. We firmly believe that operational resilience is essential to driving and sustaining responsible innovation.
Today, we mark a milestone in our ongoing commitment to the financial sector that’s directly relevant to our customers in the U.K. On July 10, the U.K. Treasury designated Google Cloud EMEA as a critical third part

cloud.title: Frontier and Center: Who evaluates the evaluations?
cloud.snippet: Editor’s note: Some of the most interesting questions in AI are being asked by information theoreticians, around how to provide context to an emerging class of AI agents. A few weeks ago, we waded into those waters with a blog about the Open Knowledge Format, a specification that formalizes the LLM-wiki pattern into a portable, interoperable format to represent the metadata, context, and curated k

cloud.title: What’s new with Google Cloud
cloud.snippet:
Want to know the latest from Google Cloud? Find it here in one handy location. Check back regularly for our newest updates, announcements, resources, events, learning opportunities, and more. 

Tip: Not sure where to find what you’re looking for on the Google Cloud blog? Start here: Google Cloud blog 101: Full list of topics, links, and resources.

cloud.title: Frontier models and production agents: Advancing Microsoft Foundry for the agentic era
cloud.snippet:
Introducing OpenAI's latest frontier model series, the Asia Pacific Data Zone, and product agent capabilities, all generally available in Microsoft Foundry.
The post Frontier models and production agents: Advancing Microsoft Foundry for the agentic era appeared first on Microsoft Azure Blog.

cloud.title: Safely run AI-generated code in Cloud Run sandboxes
cloud.snippet:
Here’s a question we hear often at Google Cloud: How do you safely run AI-generated code or untrusted binaries without putting your host application, data, and cloud credentials at risk? In other words, how do you give AI-written programs a safe space to run — one that keeps them completely separate from your trusted programs with higher privileges?
Until now, developers had to build complex sandb

cloud.title: Solve harder problems with AlphaEvolve, now available to everyone on Google Cloud
cloud.snippet: Many of the most challenging and valuable problems in the world are related to optimization. Now, AI is now making these problems tractable. If you've ever tried to design a microchip, plan a delivery network, or optimize a training architecture for a large AI model, you know how hard it is to find the most optimized code. Traditional coding methods often cannot explore all the possible algorithms

cloud.title: Autopilot Clusters with GKE managed DRANET: GPUs and TPUs
cloud.snippet:
Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) managed DRANET supports both GPUs and TPUs. There are several configurations to use this implementation, including standard cluster (where you have full control) and autopilot cluster (where Google does the heavy configs for you). I've been exploring the capabilities and in this blog we will explore setting up for autopilot clusters.


Autopilot and managed DRANET
GK

hardware.title: Amazon Prime members can get this Asus RTX 5060 for just $2 above MSRP — upgrade to Blackwell gaming power for less than the cost of an RTX 3060
hardware.snippet: Amazon is giving some Prime customers a deep discount on Asus's Prime RTX 5060 8GB OC, bringing its price to just $2 above MSRP and beating the Prime Day deals we saw on these cards.

hardware.title: Tesla's AI5 with 2nm-class node tapes out at Samsung Foundry — production starts soon, months after TSMC tape out
hardware.snippet: Samsung Foundry soon to join TSMC in production of Tesla's AI5 processor, a LinkedIn post reveals.

hardware.title: Upcoming MSI Afterburner update adds heatmap to V/F curve editor to show your GPU's boosting behavior — new feature shoots for better overclocks with more data
hardware.snippet: MSI Afterburner is soon getting a new heatmap in its V/F curve editor that shows the GPU's boosting behavior in real workloads.

hardware.title: Intel's new space-grade Starfire chip is a Panther Lake SoC that puts an 18A CPU into orbit — chip designed for the US government leverages Intel 3 for the GPU
hardware.snippet: Intel has unveiled Starfire, a space-grade system-on-chip designed for the U.S. government.

hardware.title: Cooler Master HAF II 500 Case Review: New HAF delivers on its name, with impressive airflow and a roomy chassis
hardware.snippet: Cooler Master’s HAF II 500 revives the HAF legacy with massive 220mm fans, excellent airflow, and solid thermal performance. Its cooling capability, spacious interior, flexible building options, and quiet operation make it an intriguing option for around $200.

hardware.title: Meta expands colossal Hyperion AI supercluster plans to 5GW, pushes Louisiana investment past $50 billion as AI race accelerates — says it plans to invest over $1 billion in local infrastructure improvements
hardware.snippet: Meta is expanding Hyperion from 2 GW to 5 GW, lifting its Louisiana investment above $50 billion as it races to secure more AI computing capacity.

hardware.title: AMD FSR Multi-Frame Generation with 8x mode spotted — experimental driver settings could hint at FSR's next evolution
hardware.snippet: Experimental options discovered in AMD's latest Radeon driver suggest the company is preparing next-generation FSR technologies, but there's no confirmation whether 8x Multi-Frame Generation mode will ever ship in its current form.

hardware.title: Flashforge Creator 5 review: Basic and affordable tool changer
hardware.snippet: The Flashforge Creator 5 tool changer is basic, budget, and nearly perfect.

hardware.title: Apple's rumored M7 Ultra targets 1.5TB of memory and Blackwell-class AI performance, report claims — monster 2028 offering would depend on memory shortage easing
hardware.snippet: Apple's planned M7 Ultra chip is being designed to support up to 1.5 TB of unified memory and to push AI performance toward the class of Nvidia's Blackwell accelerators.

hardware.title: Save $544 on a new RTX 5080-packed Cyberpower gaming PC — also features AMD's 9800X3D, 32GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2TB SSD for $2,744
hardware.snippet: Save over $544 on a new high-powered gaming rig from Cyberpower. High-end graphics and frame rates, thanks to the included RTX 5080 and Ryzen 7 9800X3D hardware inside.

hardware.title: Valve confirms Steam Machine red light overheating warning is showing earlier than it should; BIOS fix on the way — will raise temperature warning threshold to 100 Degrees Celsius
hardware.snippet: Valve has confirmed that the Steam Machine's red light bar warning is being triggered prematurely.

hardware.title: Grab 32GB of Corsair Vengeance DDR5 for just $236, $150 cheaper than the best standalone price — epic Newegg combo deal saves you $234 overall and comes with an AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D and a Gigabyte X870 motherboard
hardware.snippet: Get the AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB of fast DDR5 RAM, and a Gigabyte X870E motherboard for $1,064.98, with the RAM costing you just $236.

hardware.title: Lenovo's Legion 7a gaming laptop now comes with an RTX 5070 12GB GPU option — but it costs $3,375 paired with a Ryzen AI 9 CPU, SKU was previously limited to RTX 5060
hardware.snippet: Lenovo has added the RTX 5070 12GB GPU to its Legion 7a gaming laptop, allowing you finally configure it with something better than an RTX 5060. It's very expensive at $3,375 but you're getting a genuinely premium machine for that price and an upgraded RTX 5070 that's not limited to just 8GB of VRAM.

hardware.title: Ireland’s data centers consumed nearly as much electricity as every home in the country combined in 2025 — server farms gulped 23% of national power despite years of grid restrictions
hardware.snippet: Ireland’s data centers consumed 23% of the country’s electricity in 2025, rising 10% in one year despite restrictions on new grid connections.

hardware.title: Sega Dreamcast driver fixes appear in Linux 7.2-rc3 — fabled console remains in favor while iconic computing architectures like i486 fall by the wayside
hardware.snippet: A set of updates for Sega Dreamcast hardware has been merged into the Linux 7.2-rc3 kernel this weekend.

hardware.title: Grab a blazing-fast dual-interface M.2 SSD enclosure for just $59 on Amazon — Asus' tool-less ROG Strix Aiolos is 14% off right now, featuring transfer speeds up to 20 Gbps
hardware.snippet: Asus' fancy dual-interface M.2 enclosure is marked down to its lowest price ever on Amazon right now. Not only is it fast, but it also looks clean and has extra on-the-go convenience thanks to its metal hook and strap.

hardware.title: Sotheby’s video showcases working Apple-1 serial number 01-0033 — part of its upcoming History of Science & Technology sale
hardware.snippet: Sotheby’s is preparing a blockbuster History of Science & Technology sale packed with amazing artifacts and collectors’ items which includes a working Apple-1, serial number 01-0033.

hardware.title: Windows 95 didn’t detect installers, it ‘guessed’ based on the file name, says veteran dev — it simply checked for words like setup, install, inst, or localized equivalents
hardware.snippet: The full match list ran to six terms: setup, install, inst, imposta, ayarla, and felrak.

hardware.title: FCC approves orbital space mirrors, first test satellites will launch this year — large spacecraft reflects sunlight to Earth’s surface for construction sites, search-and-rescue lighting, and more
hardware.snippet: A startup that aims to bring sunlight on Earth after dark just received approval from the FCC to launch its experimental satellite. Critics say that the project could adversely affect astronomy and the environment, but the regulator says that their complaints are outside of its jursidiction.

hardware.title: Cooler Master MWE Gold 750 V4 power supply review: Verified Gold efficiency with mainstream pricing
hardware.snippet: The fourth revision of Cooler Master’s renowned mainstream series, coming with verified Gold efficiency, a native 12V-2x6 connector, and GPU Shield current monitoring in a compact 140 mm chassis at a mainstream price.

hardware.title: This 3D-printed electric motorbike folds into your luggage — creator warns it is 'super fast... way too fast'
hardware.snippet: Ivan Miranda has released the design files for the Mirandetta, a 3D-printed electric scooter that breaks down to fit inside a suitcase.

hardware.title: Hotspot temperature sensor on Nvidia's Blackwell gaming GPUs is still accessible if you have access to Nvidia's internal MODS tool — Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti caught throttling at 107°C over poor TIM application
hardware.snippet: Nvidia decided to hide the hotspot temperature on its RTX 50 series, but internal diagnostic tools, such as Nvidia's own "MODS," can still read it. The resulting data reveals how some GPUs can overheat and throttle easily, which could be why the sensor was kept hidden in the first place.

hardware.title: Newegg packs Ryzen 5 9600X and 16GB DDR5 into a $520 combo — bundles also include a B650 motherboard and 240mm AIO liquid cooler
hardware.snippet: Gamers looking to build a budget-friendly AM5 PC can pick up a Ryzen 5 9600X, a Gigabyte B650M motherboard, and a Corsair DDR5 memory bundle, with a free 240mm liquid cooler included.

hardware.title: Microsoft loses Brazilian court case after telling hacked Xbox user to re-purchase games — tech giant ordered to restore Xbox account with all games and pay $400 in damages
hardware.snippet: A Brazilian gamer who lost his Microsoft account and all his digital games has won a court order requiring the company to return them.

hardware.title: Bambu Lab collabs with Insta360 for epic design contest — win thousands in 3D printers, Luna Ultra cameras, and gift cards
hardware.snippet: Your design concept could win a next-gen camera and 3D printer.

hardware.title: AMD RX 9070 GRE collapses to $499 to save 1440p gaming — RDNA 4 price slips 9% to steal a piece of Nvidia's mid-range pie
hardware.snippet: AMD's Radeon RX 9070 GRE has received its first price cut since launching outside China, making the 1440p-focused RDNA 4 graphics card a more compelling alternative to Nvidia's RTX 5060 Ti 16GB.

hardware.title: SK Hynix says 2027 will be the 'worst year' for memory shortage, forecasts crunch to last until 2030 — CEO shares grim outlook on the day SK Hynix gets listed on Nasdaq
hardware.snippet: SK Hynix CEO Kwak Noh-jung says the memory shortage will get even worse in 2027, and claiming the RAM crunch will last at least until the turn of the decade.

hardware.title: Microsoft struggles to fulfill its 2030 sustainability promise amid carbon-heavy AI expansions — the company's chief sustainability officer claims the target is still feasible
hardware.snippet: Microsoft's carbon emissions jumped 25% in FY2025 as AI data center expansion outpaced sustainability gains, despite progress in water conservation and waste reduction.

hardware.title: Razer Soma Chroma Gaming Chair Review: Light on adjustability, but heavy on RGBs
hardware.snippet: If you’d like a dose of RGBs to go with your gaming chair, the Soma Chroma delivers for $499.

hardware.title: Colibrì proof-of-concept gains frontier-level 1.5-TB AI model — novel approach runs on only 25GB of RAM and shows promise for local AI setups
hardware.snippet: Colibrì proof-of-concept gets a frontier-level AI model running on only 25 GB of RAM and a modest CPU

hardware.title: Fake Go DNS scanner spread malware through over 200 GitHub repos — 'Operation Muck and Load' has published 700 malicious modules since January
hardware.snippet: The module published its first version on January 24 this year and has since accumulated more than 1,200 versions.

hardware.title: Flock cameras mistakenly track car reviewer over 'stolen' tags — police ambush tester in store parking lot and detain him for an hour
hardware.snippet: Flock AI cameras failed to read the smaller digits on a non-standard New Jersey plate, leading cops to block in the driver on suspicion of driving a vehicle with "stolen" tags. It turns out the initial police report omitted the smaller numbers, resulting in several legitimate plates getting flagged by the system.

hardware.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged theft of trade secrets — claims company mentored incoming employees on bringing confidential information
hardware.snippet: Apple sued OpenAI, including its own former employees, over the theft of trade secrets as both companies build up AI hardware businesses.

hardware.title: SK hynix and TetraMem collaborate on experimental chip to bolster energy efficiency for edge AI devices — memristor-based in-memory SoC research leaves performance questions up in the air
hardware.snippet: SK hynix, TetraMem, and the University of Southern California built a memristor-based in-memory computing system-on-chip for AI edge devices, achieving promising energy efficiency, but failed to demonstrate its full potential.

hardware.title: Anthropic says it can read Claude's 'thoughts,' as detailed in new research paper — models observed to have a global workspace, revealing more of what makes LLMs tick
hardware.snippet: Anthropic has discovered an internal "J-space" for its Claude AI that displays similarities to human internal processing. While the AI developer anthropomorphizes it as thought, it may yet prove useful as a method of improving LLM honesty, oversight, and guardrails.

hardware.title: Intel's midrange Core Ultra 5 245K is down to its lowest price ever at just $179 on Amazon — save up to 42% on a solid gaming CPU with 14 cores and PCIe 5.0 support
hardware.snippet: Intel's forgotten 14-core SKU from last year has received a sizable discount on Amazon, making it one of the best value propositions in CPUs right now. It performs amicably in gaming and professional tasks thanks to its 6 P-cores and 8 E-cores that can clock up to 5.2 GHz.

hardware.title: Steam sales reportedly topped $11 billion during H1 2026 due to shifting trends — staggering growth driven by influx of Chinese players and booming legacy catalogues
hardware.snippet: Steam made an estimated $11.1 billion in revenue in the first six months of 2026, according to estimates from research firm Alinea Analytics. That's more than it did in the entire pandemic-ridden year of 2020. In fact, even last year's holiday season made 8% less money than H1 2026, making this half Valve's best-ever.

hardware.title: Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026) Review: Stunning Mini‑LED, serious muscle, and a few missed steps
hardware.snippet: The Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 pairs an 18-inch mini-LED display with cutting-edge components, but omissions like PCIe 5.0 storage and dual-channel RAM —plus slightly weaker performance than Razer’s Blade 18 —keep it from taking top honors.

hardware.title: Tencent is reportedly in talks to acquire Manus from Meta, following Beijing intervention — company expects to remain independent of Chinese tech giant
hardware.snippet: Tencent is in talks with Manus and other investors to raise the $2 billion needed to buy back the startup from Meta. Beijing ordered the two companies to unwind the deal six months after the surprise announcement of its purchase.

hardware.title: SK hynix raises a record $26.5 billion in historic U.S. IPO — South Korean memory giant to fund massive HBM manufacturing expansions
hardware.snippet: SK hynix raised $26.5 billion in a record-breaking Nasdaq IPO, as it plans to channel the windfall from surging AI demand and sold-out HBM supply to fund new fabs.

hardware.title: Nanya to quadruple capital spending to $6.2 billion in 2027 as DRAM prices push gross margin to 79.5% — Q2 revenue skyrockets as ASPs for memory continue to surge
hardware.snippet: Nanya Technology plans capex of more than TW$200 billion ($6.2 billion) in 2027, roughly four times its budget for this year.

hardware.title: Japanese chipmaker Rapidus to offer lower wafer pricing than TSMC — 2nm class silicon to be priced around $20,000 on 2027 launch
hardware.snippet: Japanese chipmaker Rapidus discloses one more aspect of its strategy: to offer lower quotes than TSMC.

hardware.title: Save 84% on a two-year ExpressVPN subscription, offering four additional months for free — upgrade your privacy for under $70 with no-logs access to servers in 105 countries worldwide
hardware.snippet: Right now, you'll save $378 in total on over two years' worth of ExpressVPN, now priced at $69.72, with four extra months thrown in for free.

hardware.title: Researchers turn HBM on its side to tackle AI memory’s heat wall — Korean V-Die and Japanese MOSAIC designs promise higher bandwidth, denser stacks, and cooler future GPUs
hardware.snippet: Researchers in Korea and Japan have proposed sideways-stacked DRAM designs that could push future AI memory beyond conventional HBM limits by improving cooling, bandwidth, and capacity while reducing reliance on TSV-heavy vertical stacks.

hardware.title: Logitech's MX Master 4 hits $102 at Lenovo — up your productivity game with haptic feedback and effortless scrolling
hardware.snippet: Pick up the fantastic Logitech MX Master 4 productivity mouse from Lenovo and make a saving when you stack these two Lenovo e-coupon codes.

hardware.title: Chat Control 1.0 sneaks through the EU Parliament, letting companies scan user data without warrants — legal tactic used to force a majority-required re-vote on eve of Parliament break
hardware.snippet: Chat Control 1.0 sneaks through the EU Parliament, letting companies scan user data without warrants — legal skullduggery used to force a majority-required re-vote on eve of Parliament break

hardware.title: Micron lifts U.S. spending to $250 billion — company takes $500 million position in America's only 300 mm wafer plant
hardware.snippet: Micron has said it will invest up to $3 billion in the US semiconductor supply chain, with $500 million of that going to GlobalWafers.

hardware.title: Samsung readies Gaia AI accelerator for PCs — HP and Lenovo are reportedly validating the NPU
hardware.snippet: Samsung reportedly preps Gaia AI accelerator for client devices that is already being tested by HP and Lenovo.

hardware.title: Minecraft shown running on Game Boy Color and Game Boy in 3D with textures — developer coaxed 3D look out of barely-there hardware
hardware.snippet: Because getting it to run on the Game Boy Advance clearly wasn't hard enough.

hardware.title: TeamGroup G70 Pro 2TB SSD Review: Low latency meets affordable DRAM
hardware.snippet: The TeamGroup G70 Pro is a high-end drive without a high-end price. Good performance, but poor power efficiency keeps it in check.

hardware.title: Redditor buys suspicious drives on eBay just to report the scamming sellers if they get a fake SSD or HDD — latest '16TB' find has weights and microSD card hot-glued inside the enclosure to make it feel legit
hardware.snippet: u/Hartkralle says that eBay refunds them when they report these fake drives, so getting scammers banned from the platform is worth their effort. While fake sellers would likely just create a new account on eBay in an hour or so, they say that it's still another hour before an unsuspecting victim buys these fraudulent items.

hardware.title: Ingenious father fixes dead RTX 3070 with a jerry-rigged capacitor from an old radio — Saves worried son $120 in repair costs, GPU 'works better than before' now
hardware.snippet: A Russian family has just saved the house $120 in GPU repairs after the father fixed it with a salvaged capacitor from an old radio.

hardware.title: While the U.S. flip-flops on chip sanctions, China is building its own chip supply market — export controls are creating conditions for a Sino-Russian chip trade alliance
hardware.snippet: As the U.S. makes up its mind on export controls for Chinese chips, China has been developing its own supply chain, and associated trade network.

hardware.title: AMD revives aging Zen 2 processor for budget PCs — Ryzen 7 4700LE resurfaces in a new $800 RTX 3050 prebuilt
hardware.snippet: AMD's quiet revival of older Ryzen processors continues, with the Ryzen 7 4700LE now appearing in a prebuilt gaming desktop priced at $799.99.

hardware.title: Intel preps 28-core Nova Lake-S CPUs for Dunlow workstation platform — Entry-level Xeon chip features LGA1954 socket
hardware.snippet: Intel readies Xeon 'Dunlow' platform with 28 cores in LGA1954 packaging for entry-level servers and workstations.

hardware.title: Nvidia shows off GeForce Trading Cards Series 1 — collectible cards show off games, GPUs, and tech demos, and will be available for free at upcoming events
hardware.snippet: Nvidia is creating a set of collectible trading cards that will be given away for free during live events and giveaways this summer.

hardware.title: Professor suspected AI-powered cheating on take-home midterms, makes finals in-person — only two students scored within 10% of their midterm score
hardware.snippet: A Brown University professor suspected that almost his entire class cheated on take-home mid-term exams using AI tools after they scored unusually high. In-person final exams showed that only two students scored within 10% of their midterm score, with just one getting a higher grade compared to their midterms.

hardware.title: Sega’s $5M investment saved Nvidia in 1996, now Jensen Huang is heading to Tokyo to mark 30 years of partnership — Akihabara event will include a GeForce RTX 5090 FE lottery, an RTX Spark presentation, and more
hardware.snippet: Nvidia and Sega have scheduled an event next week to celebrate their history and longstanding friendship.

hardware.title: Alienware AW3426DW gaming monitor review: Premium gaming and OLED goodness in a value-priced package
hardware.snippet: Alienware delivers value from a 34-inch ultra-wide OLED with the AW3426DW. This WQHD curved screen sports Quantum Dot wide gamut color, HDR500, Dolby Vision, 280 Hz, and Adaptive-Sync.

hardware.title: Save 32% on this Samsung 1440p gaming monitor with a fast 240Hz refresh rate, now $169 — score this 27-inch IPS display upgrade with a fast 200Hz refresh rate for your gaming PC with an $80 discount
hardware.snippet: This 27-inch Samsung Odyssey G53F gaming monitor is on sale for $169.99 right now, offering a 1440p resolution and fast 200Hz refresh rate at a great price.

hardware.title: AMD's upcoming Zen 6 Medusa Point 10-core APU pops up on Geekbench — chip is faster than Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 & even Ryzen AI Max+ 395
hardware.snippet: A new 10-core engineering sample from AMD has surfaced on Geekbench, being identified as part of the Medusa Point family. It's likely the Ryzen AI 9 565 and its scores easily beat the Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 and even the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 in certain benchmarks.

hardware.title: Elon Musk receives FTC greenlight to buy Mesh Optical as interconnects emerge as AI's tightest bottleneck — the move will expand Musk's growing stack of critical AI infrastructure
hardware.snippet: FTC clearance to acquire Mesh Optical hands Musk the missing layer between Terafab's chips and Gigasat's satellites, amid tightening interconnect AI bottleneck

hardware.title: New hack exploits AI hallucinations to trick agents into running malicious code — 'HalluSquatting' attack exploits a fundamental weakness in every available model
hardware.snippet: Attackers can exploit how AI bots hallucinate software URLs to create massive botnets. The vulnerability is endemic to every model.

hardware.title: Future Nostalgia Project asks retro hoarders to ‘Copy That Floppy!’ — flips the early 1990s anti-piracy campaign on its head to encourage budding archivists
hardware.snippet: Flipping the infamous early 1990s anti-piracy campaign messaging on its head, the Future Nostalgia Project is asking retro hoarders to Copy That Floppy!

hardware.title: Save $60 on this Hoto DIY and PC maintenance bundle with electric screwdriver and 4-in-1 air duster — just $89 for popular USB-C rechargeable driver with 25 bits, along with a separate blower and vacuum cleaner to keep your setup clean
hardware.snippet: Save nearly $61 on this two-for-one Hoto combo deal at Amazon right now, giving you the NEX O1 Pro mini electric screwdriver set with 25 bits with its AutoCare 4-in-1 air duster and vacuum cleaner in a combo Amazon deal for under $90.

hardware.title: AMD EXPO ULL shows middling performance gains in initial tests despite eye-watering price increase — first benchmarks show up to a 4% improvement with DDR5-6000 CL36
hardware.snippet: The first independent benchmarks for AMD's EXPO ULL memory are available, showing just up to a 4% improvement despite an increase in price.

hardware.title: AI servers will consume more power than all conventional data center hardware combined by 2027 — global data center electricity consumption set to grow by 26% this year, Gartner forecasts
hardware.snippet: Global data center electricity consumption will grow 26% in 2026 to reach 565 TWh, up from 447 TWh in 2025.

hardware.title: Chinese courts allow heirs to inherit accounts of deceased gamers — multiple cases spanning years establish precedent for digital ownership of games, in-game items, and microtransactions
hardware.snippet: A Reddit user named u/Slawrfp shared on the r/pcmasterrace subreddit that Chinese courts have allowed heirs to inherit games and other digital assets after the original user has since passed on. While Chinese inheritance law hasn't explicitly covered digital properties, multiple rulings have already set precedent.

quantum.title: Quantum Companies Help Develop Hybrid AI for Immune-Targeting Peptides
quantum.snippet: Insider Brief A hybrid quantum-classical artificial intelligence system generated short protein fragments that attached to immune-system proteins, with the largest gains appearing for genetic variants that are poorly represented in existing data, according to a preprint posted on bioRxiv. The findings suggest quantum-generated patterns could help AI systems search more effectively for vaccine and

quantum.title: Why Quantum Computing Needs Strong Ecosystems to Scale
quantum.snippet: Guest Post by Yonathan Cohen, Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer of Quantum Machines. Something is changing in the quantum industry. Not long ago, most conversations focused almost entirely on qubits: fidelities, coherence times, error rates, and which hardware modality might eventually win. quantum.title: Top Diamond NV-Centre Quantum Computing Companies in 2026

quantum.title: Google Study Shows Quantum Computer Can Learn From Its Own Errors While It Computes
quantum.snippet: Insider Brief A Google-led research team has demonstrated a quantum computer that continuously learns from its own errors while it is running, replacing one of the biggest operational bottlenecks in quantum computing with an artificial intelligence system that adapts as conditions change. The study, published in Nature, describes a reinforcement learning system that uses the […]

quantum.title: Google Seeks University Proposals for Early Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing
quantum.snippet: Insider Brief Google is seeking university researchers to help solve two of quantum computing’s most immediate challenges, launching academic funding calls focused on developing useful algorithms for early fault-tolerant quantum computers and securing the systems that control them. The new funding opportunities, announced as part of Google’s Academic Research Awards program, reflect two priorities

education.title: Translating Higher Education Cybersecurity Into Business Value
education.snippet:
For years, cybersecurity has been treated as a technical problem and an unavoidable cost of doing business. In higher education, that framing is no longer workable. Universities are competing for students, grants and reputation in an environment where outages and breaches have direct consequences on retention, research dollars and institutional credibility.
The conversation has shifted. Cybersecur

education.title: Rethinking Managed Security Services in Higher Ed: From Ticket Closers to Strategic Partners
education.snippet:
Managed Security Service Providers used to be seen as a way to “outsource the pain” of monitoring and incident response. For many universities — where small teams are tasked with protecting sprawling, open and highly connected environments — that was reason enough to consider them.
But in today’s threat landscape, simply shipping logs to a vendor and measuring them by ticket closure rates is no lo

education.title: Children learn best on paper: We need better technology, not less of it
education.snippet: It is an odd thing to hear from someone who builds education technology for a living, but the most important tool in an elementary classroom is still a pencil. The learning science says so.

education.title: Future-Ready Strategies for Technology Leaders in Higher Education
education.snippet:
Higher education technology leaders face an increasingly difficult balancing act. Enrollment pressures, tighter budgets and rising expectations around artificial intelligence (AI) are forcing institutions to modernize while proving the value of every technology investment. At the same time, aging infrastructure, fragmented data and staffing constraints leave little room for missteps.

education.title: Rethinking MTSS: How schools can turn student data into meaningful support
education.snippet: Schools today are navigating an unprecedented convergence of academic gaps, behavioral challenges, chronic absenteeism and rising student mental health needs.

education.title: 5 Questions About Security Debt for Higher Ed Institutions
education.snippet:
Technical debt is the accumulation of future costs that come with every IT product in your portfolio. For many IT managers, managing technical debt is a careful balancing act to ensure expenditures are predictable and problems are avoided. Security debt is a variation on technical debt — and a bigger problem in higher education.

education.title: Schools need SEL assessments to fight chronic absenteeism
education.snippet: Our schools face a paradoxical challenge: chronic absenteeism, which is being treated as an attendance problem, actually isn’t.
